name: Peru Copper, Inc.
description: Peru Copper Inc. was a Vancouver-based mining company primarily focused on the acquisition and exploration of copper deposits in Peru. The company is best known for its development of the Toromocho copper-molybdenum project located in the Junin department of central Peru. In 2007, the company was acquired by the Aluminum Corporation of China (Chinalco) in a transaction valued at approximately $840 million. Following this acquisition, Peru Copper Inc. ceased to operate as an independent publicly traded entity, and its shares were delisted from the Toronto Stock Exchange.
